title: [v4.4.1-rhel] Bump runc to 1.2.9, Buildah to v1.29.6
body:
Addresses CVE-2025-52881, and also CVE-2025-31133 and CVE-2025-52565

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126910, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126908, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-64908, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-64909, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-64907, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-64906

Also addresses CVE-2025-65637.

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-67547, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-67730, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-67897, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-68036, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-135812, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-135827

Also addresses: "Bump to runc v1.2.9 or v1.3.4 to get CVE and regression fixes - Podman - [OCP 4.*]

Fixes: https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-66313, https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-66312, https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-66311, https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/OCPBUGS-66310
 
Replaces: #27764

Formerly titled "Frankenstein".

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
This is a massive rework of Podman (480k+ lines of changes), necessitated by changes to runc and other dependencies.  We don't anticipate any changes for the end user based on our testing; our focus throughout this process has been to keep your experience exactly as it is today. While the engine under the hood has been significantly modernized to stay ahead of industry standards, the commands, scripts, and workflows you rely on daily should continue to function as normal.
```

comments.body: See https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27764 for history . This draft includes fixes for integration tests. Remote integration not tried yet.

I'll start a RHEL 9.2 system test run for this, and include a scratch build of buildah I did yesterday which includes all the runc stuffs...

Edit: I'll do this with `[engine]\nruntime = "runc"` since that makes more sense than defaulting to crun.

Local rootless results on my RHEL 9.2 setup:

[output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24946392/output.txt)

The ps --external test is still failing (despite me updating buildah and runc) :sob: I really don't know what to make of this.  All the other failures are expected, and obviously environment or test bugs.

I just did what I probably should have done earlier: Run the commands in the test by hand.  The result: **It passes!**

[manual_ps_external.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24947377/manual_ps_external.txt)

All I did was:

1. Stop the automation right before it executed the tests
2. ssh in as root, create "testuser1234"
3. ssh in as testuser1234
4. run the equivalent commands from the test, one by one.

So I'm even more convinced that podman and buildah are fine, it's just the way I'm running our tests (or the environment) that's (somehow) the problem.  I'm okay with leaving that up to future-me to investigate and fix.

Ran 3a48ef139c40aef0cf7719c3edd7896b6901c41c through RHEL 9.2 system tests with an updated runc, crun, and buildah.  One concerning result(*) that may deserve further review, the rest looks similar to previous runs:

[local_root_system.log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25024653/local_root_system.log.txt)

- I'm Ignoring failures due to unexpected runc output and missing `getfattr` command.
- `pod resource limits` failure *(looks bad)
- `podman auto-update - label io.containers.autoupdate=image` (broken test?)
- `podman update - test all options` (broken test?)

[local_rootless_system.log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25025532/local_rootless_system.log.txt)

- I'm Ignoring failures due to unexpected runc output.
- `podman sigproxy test: <XYZ>` (timeouts, likely broken tests)
- `podman ps --external` (same as before)
- Pasta tests - no IPv6 in test environment, so most of them break.

For comparison sake, I ran the tests again from _"Fix storage container mount check to fail on errors"_ (932481e76ec40676131610376504883a6d840876) from #27764 (Note: different from this PR based on ID).  Here are the results:

[932481e_local_root_system.log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25048909/932481e_local_root_system.log.txt)

- `podman run - basic tests` can't handle runc output (test bug / same as [before](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987#issuecomment-3837531874))
- `podman exec - basic test` "" "" "" (test bug / same as [before](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987#issuecomment-3837531874))
- `pod resource limits` - same as [before](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987#issuecomment-3837531874)
- `podman update - test all options` - same as [before](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987#issuecomment-3837531874)  maybe Fedora/RHEL difference?
- SELinux failures due to missing `getfattr` were fixed by installing `attr` package.

[932481e_local_rootless_system.log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25049877/932481e_local_rootless_system.log.txt)

- Same run/exec basic test failures as "rootfull" (above)
- Same sigproxy failures (timeout waiting for BYE) as previous runs
- Same mystery `podman ps --external` failure (see below)
- Pasta failures appear the same as before (no IPv6 availability on VM)

---

**Update on the `podman ps --external` weirdness:**  While the semi-automated run was happening, I separately ssh'd into the same VM as a (new) `testuser`.  I transcribed the BATS test into a simple bash script, and ran it repeatedly in a loop -- It never failed.

[ps_external.sh.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25049884/ps_external.sh.txt)

Next I modified the script to simply create a bunch of "external" containers with mounts (i.e. never cleaning up after itself) and ran that a few dozen times.  Un-modified the script and ran it -- speculating that maybe something leftover was causing the problem.  Alas, it still passes.

Lastly I manually went into the `/usr/share/podman/test/system` directory and ran the ultimate shell command used by our test system, with only the addition of specifically running the `ps` tests only: `$ PATH=/root/.local/bin:/root/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/libexec/podman     CI_DESIRED_NETWORK=netavark PODMAN_TESTING=/usr/bin/podman-testing RELEASE_TESTING=true    bats --tap 040-ps.bats`  This also passes without error.  So I ran it in a loop, but it consistently passes.

Yet when the TMT-based test system orchestrates everything, this test consistently fails.  The only other theory I have is that perhaps it has something to do with the penultimate command used to start the tests _from the root account_:

```
su - notroot -c '<test command above>'.
```

Unfortunately I was not able to try this in time before the semi-automated tests concluded and the VM was clobbered :disappointed:

Confirmed: The `ps --external` test fails (repeatedly and consistently) because our TMT-based execution environment runs it via `su`:

```
[root@system ~]# su - notroot -c 'eval whoami && cd /usr/share/podman/test/system && PATH=/root/.local/bin:/root/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/libexec/podman CI_DESIRED_NETWORK=netavark PODMAN_TESTING=/usr/bin/podman-testing RELEASE_TESTING=true bats --tap 040-ps.bats'
notroot
1..3
ok 1 podman ps - basic tests
ok 2 podman ps --filter
not ok 3 podman ps --external
# (from function `die' in file helpers.bash, line 558,
#  from function `run_podman' in file helpers.bash, line 228,
#  in test file 040-ps.bats, line 147)
#   `run_podman 2 rm "$cid"' failed
...cut...
# $ podman ps --external
# CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND     CREATED                 STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
# 23be26a5f1da  quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018  buildah     Less than a second ago  Storage                 testimage-working-container
# $ podman rm 23be26a5f1da
# 23be26a5f1da
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 0; expected 2
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

Subsequently, if I then run the same command directly via ssh'ing into the VM as the `notroot` user, the test continues to fails consistently.  However, after a `podman system reset --force` and `rm -rf .local .config` (both as `notroot`) "clears" this state!  Now running the test (directly via ssh as `notroot`) it consistently passes.

So I did the experiment again, capturing the output of `podman system info` for each state.  Then ran a diff on the output, and located what is probably the smoking gun:

```
[notroot@system ~]$ diff -u podman_system_info-after_su.txt podman_system_info-after_reset.txt
...cut...boring...stuff...
-  runRoot: /tmp/containers-user-1000/containers
+  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
```

Indeed, if I reset/rm again, and set `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` in the `su` command, the test now passes consistently:

```
[root@system ~]# su - notroot -c 'eval whoami && cd /usr/share/podman/test/system && PATH=/root/.local/bin:/root/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/libexec/podman CI_DESIRED_NETWORK=netavark PODMAN_TESTING=/usr/bin/podman-testing RELEASE_TESTING=true XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000 bats --tap 040-ps.bats'
```

:heavy_check_mark: PASS :heavy_check_mark:

Tested [0d19500](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987/commits/0d1950030f959294dfe70ac9be8449e43aef262a), the results are unchanged with one exception:  The system is now setting `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` for the rootless variant, so the `ps --external` test passed.

Testing of [044d71e](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987/commits/044d71ea851cfe66d7dd328ac0d28a42f4e1ab54) is still ongoing, since I'm also running the "remote" tests this time.  The local results are ready though.

[044d71e-local_rootless_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25102947/044d71e-local_rootless_system.txt)
[044d71e-local_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25102948/044d71e-local_root_system.txt)

By my :eye: these appear unchanged compared to prior runs.  @lsm5 since you're fixing system tests, any thoughts on patching the string/exit code mismatches resulting from running w/ runc?  Specifically these two I'm seeing over and over:

```
not ok 39 podman run - basic tests
# (from function `is' in file ./helpers.bash, line 706,
#  in test file ./030-run.bats, line 39)
#   `is "$output" "$expected_output" "podman run $cmd - output"' failed
...cut...
# $ podman run quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 /etc
# Error: OCI runtime error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: exec: "/etc": is a directory
# [ rc=126 (expected) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #|     FAIL: podman run /etc - output
# #| expected: 'Error:.*exec.*permission denied' (using expr)
# #|   actual: 'Error: OCI runtime error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: exec: "/etc": is a directory'
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
#
```

and

```
not ok 201 podman exec - basic test
# (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 558,
#  from function `run_podman' in file ./helpers.bash, line 228,
#  in test file ./075-exec.bats, line 25)
#   `run_podman 126 exec $cid /etc' failed
...cut...
# $ podman exec 70b3f9c02e132569a91c0641415b548f5dc298315bc7caf92cf22413b57bddce /etc
# Error: OCI runtime error: runc: exec failed: unable to start container process: exec: "/etc": is a directory
# [ rc=255 (** EXPECTED 126 **) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 255; expected 126
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
#
```

Though perhaps the fact that one exits 126 and the other 125 is also unexpected behavior?

Remote results (nothing new/exciting), basically the same as the "local" tests by my :eye:

[044d71e-remote_rootless_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25110927/044d71e-remote_rootless_system.txt)
[044d71e-remote_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25110928/044d71e-remote_root_system.txt)
@cevich some more updates. PTAL at the latest.

> @cevich some more updates. PTAL at the latest.

The run and exec failures in rootful should be fixed with the latest. I have yet to run them as rootless, but please give it a try.

RHEL 9.2 system test results:

- The local rootless tests are all passing now.  The only exception are pasta tests that require IPv6 which is non-functional/unavailable in this environment.
- The local root tests had two failures.  One was IPv6 related so can be ignored.  The other I think it's related to the "test/system: adjust pod memory limit for runc minimum requirement" commit changes:
```
not ok 305 pod resource limits
# (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 558,
#  from function `run_podman' in file ./helpers.bash, line 228,
#  in test file ./200-pod.bats, line 528)
#   `run_podman --cgroup-manager=$cgm pod start $name' failed
# # /usr/bin/podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# # /usr/bin/podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
# # /usr/bin/podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
# quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 f5a99120db64
# # /usr/bin/podman --cgroup-manager=systemd pod create --name=resources-systemd --cpus=5 --memory=6m --memory-swa
p=1g --cpu-shares=1000 --cpuset-cpus=0 --cpuset-mems=0 --device-read-bps=/dev/loop0:1mb --device-write-bps=/dev/lo
op0:1mb --blkio-weight=50
# 551c5819d262c87fdf0914b1803391b658e6b32acc6631ff697beec2e764530e
# # /usr/bin/podman --cgroup-manager=systemd pod start resources-systemd
# /usr/lib/bats-core/test_functions.bash: line 254: warning: command substitution: ignored null byte in input
# Error: starting container d01c900ad0a41cc47798fc9f42021bdfa8502721618f81a2d57d921db40e5c67: container create fai
led (no logs from conmon): conmon bytes "": readObjectStart: expect { or n, but found , error found in #0 byte of
...||..., bigger context ...||...
# [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
- remote root also had a single failure in `podman-remote: non-default connection`
  [fedda4a_remote_root.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25135231/fedda4a_remote_root.txt)
  This is the same as I saw before, where it seems the test is expecting a default connection 
  to be absent.  I haven't reviewed the test code, but on the surface that logic seems flawed.
- remote rootless was nothing but the expected ip6 pasta failures

> * The local root tests had two failures.  One was IPv6 related so can be ignored.  The other I think it's related to the "test/system: adjust pod memory limit for runc minimum requirement" commit changes:
>
Fixed 
> 
> * remote root also had a single failure in `podman-remote: non-default connection`
>   [fedda4a_remote_root.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25135231/fedda4a_remote_root.txt)

This seems like a flake as it passed on manual run on testing-farm instance. Pushed another commit to unset PODMAN_SOCKET . Hopefully it passes for you too :crossed_fingers:
Starting another run vs [1720520](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27987/commits/172052023c772f346bc2615dd9a885b8682fe5ae)

Results:

- [1720520_local_rootless_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25191751/1720520_local_rootless_system.txt) :heavy_check_mark: Only the expected ipv6/pasta problems we can ignore*. 
- [1720520_local_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25191759/1720520_local_root_system.txt) :heavy_check_mark: 
- [1720520_remote_rootless_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25191786/1720520_remote_rootless_system.txt) :heavy_check_mark: Same as local_rootless*
- [1720520_remote_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25191802/1720520_remote_root_system.txt) :heavy_check_mark:
 
---

*Note: I _just_ noticed one of the pasta failures doesn't explicitly/directly indicate in its output anything about local IPv6 unavailability.  I was assuming it was related, but perhaps not?

```
not ok 505 podman networking with pasta(1) - ICMPv6 echo request
# (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 558,
#  from function `run_podman' in file ./helpers.bash, line 228,
#  in test file ./505-networking-pasta.bats, line 672)
#   `run_podman run --net=pasta $IMAGE ping -6 -c3 -W1 \' failed
# $ podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
# 0419269548ecd393edf55500b0eba715908b2356e272037457acb9ce19a3a73c
# $ podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
# $ podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
# quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 f5a99120db64
# $ podman run --net=pasta quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 ping -6 -c3 -W1 sh -c ping
 -c3 -W1 sed -nr "s/^nameserver[ ]{1,}([^:]*):(.*)/\1:\2/p" /etc/resolv.conf | head -1
# ping: invalid number 'ping -c3 -W1 sed -nr "s/^nameserver[ ]{1,}([^:]*):(.*)/\1:\2/p"
 /etc/resolv.conf | head -1'
# [ rc=1 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

Looking at `505-networking-pasta.bats`, line 672, this makes no sense to me at all.  Neither does the IPv4 version of the test :confused: 

Confirmed, I ran the test commands manually -- both the `podman networking with pasta(1) - ICMP echo request` are broken.

1. The IPv4 version results in a busybox usage message as output, but then it exits 0.  So BATS is treating this as a passing test.
2. The IPv4 version fails with exactly the output as seen from BATS above:  `ping: invalid number`.

I believe this is what was intended (I tried the IPv4 one and it works):

```
    run_podman run --net=pasta $IMAGE \
        sh -c 'ping -c3 -W1 $(sed -nr "s/^nameserver[ ]{1,}([^.]*).(.*)/\1.\2/p" /etc/resolv.conf | head -1)'

```

and

```
    run_podman run --net=pasta $IMAGE \
        sh -c 'ping -6 -c3 -W1 $(sed -nr "s/^nameserver[ ]{1,}([^:]*):(.*)/\1:\2/p" /etc/resolv.conf | head -1)'

```
@cevich cherrypicked 2 commits for that. PTAL.

Thanks @lsm5.  I noticed the "pasta: Fix ICMPv6 Echo test, skip it for the moment" fix is just skipping the test.  So I looked on _main_ and these tests have been removed.  So I think this is fine for now, and no reason to block this PR.

I didn't do much other searching, but if I had to guess, it probably has something to do with busybox ping exiting 0 on error :nauseated_face: Regardless, I think we should move forward.  Thanks again for all your efforts on this.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @dashea PTAL
@lsm5 can we change the title of this to something closer to the one @cevich put together please?
@lsm5 I realized I could tweak the title, I've done so and also added to the description.

> I'm not sure how one reviews a PR this size well.

We just have to do the best we can and rely heavily on testing IMHO.  If we didn't get it right, we'll adjust and release fixes as needed (assuming they're critical).

> I'm assuming that this has passed all tests

Summary:  
- Lokesh got the e2e tests running and passing locally.  He mentioned they helped find a few bugs, which he fixed.
- I was doing at least one, sometimes two system-test runs per day on RHEL 9.2 for the last two weeks.
- The system test environment needed some tweaking (forcing `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000`) to get `podman ps --external` to pass.  But IIRC this is a long-standing "issue" with the `/tmp/...` default behavior from containers/common(?) when it's unset due to running podman under `su`.
- The only other RHEL 9.2 system test failures were with 37 pasta IPv6 tests.  With two exceptions Lokesh fixed, there are many failures due to the "wacky IPv6 setup of the VM".  I have no control over that setup so I ignored the failures (all other 50+ pasta tests passed).

> @dashea what do you think.

Given the `+390,798 -89,274`-ness of this PR I really have no way of assessing this beyond the tests, so LGTM

> * Lokesh got the e2e tests running and passing locally.

They passed on a RHEL-9.2.0-Nightly testing-farm infra too. Local and remote tests. Also, apiv2 and docker compose tests passed.
Should "TBD" be in the "Release Notes" section?  Just mentioning it in case it screws up some automation or another.

> Should "TBD" be in the "Release Notes" section? Just mentioning it in case it screws up some automation or another.

Just left it as a placeholder. I'll defer to @TomSweeneyRedHat and/or you.
Maybe just the runc cve fix note?
@lsm5 @cevich I replaced the TBD in the release notes section.  WDYT?

> @lsm5 @cevich I replaced the TBD in the release notes section. WDYT?

LGTM :crossed_fingers:
Merging!
